methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Orbital Composites is a Seed-stage company in Robotics & Manufacturing founded by Cole Nielsen that has raised $6.6M to date. The comparable universe spans 9 private companies and 1 public company (277810.KQ) in the sector, though narrow peer filtering by stage and valuation tier yielded insufficient data for median-based triangulation.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement. Using $2M as a proxy for total capital deployed, this method implies a $6M valuation. A peer-median approach was not applicable; while 11 same-sector robotics companies exist, fewer than 3 satisfy simultaneous filters for stage proximity and valuation tier, making sector-wide median comparison unreliable. The top private comparables identified include 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5M to $7M, with a median of $6M. This range reflects uncertainty inherent in single-method triangulation and the absence of tightly filtered peer data. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific company's risk profile and growth trajectory, and whether the broader robotics sector comps—though available—represent genuinely comparable risk-return profiles for a Seed-stage capital equipment or software play.
